verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem derident me juniores tempore,  quorum non dignabar patres ponere cum canibus gregis mei :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֤ה ׀ שָׂ֥חֲק֣וּ עָלַי֮ צְעִירִ֥ים מִמֶּ֗נִּי לְיָ֫מִ֥ים אֲשֶׁר־מָאַ֥סְתִּי אֲבוֹתָ֑ם לָ֝שִׁ֗ית עִם־כַּלְבֵ֥י צֹאנִֽי׃
verses.king_james: But now they that are younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to have set with the dogs of my flock.

verses.latin_vulgate: quorum virtus manuum mihi erat pro nihilo,  et vita ipsa putabantur indigni :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־כֹּ֣חַ יְ֭דֵיהֶם לָ֣מָּה לִּ֑י עָ֝לֵ֗ימוֹ אָ֣בַד כָּֽלַח׃
verses.king_james: Yea, whereto might the strength of their hands profit me, in whom old age was perished?

verses.latin_vulgate: egestate et fame steriles, qui rodebant in solitudine,  squallentes calamitate et miseria.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּחֶ֥סֶר וּבְכָפָ֗ן גַּ֫לְמ֥וּד הַעֹרְקִ֥ים צִיָּ֑ה אֶ֝֗מֶשׁ שׁוֹאָ֥ה וּמְשֹׁאָֽה׃
verses.king_james: For want and famine they were solitary; fleeing into the wilderness in former time desolate and waste.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mandebant herbas, et arborum cortices,  et radix juniperorum erat cibus eorum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַקֹּטְפִ֣ים מַלּ֣וּחַ עֲלֵי־שִׂ֑יחַ וְשֹׁ֖רֶשׁ רְתָמִ֣ים לַחְמָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Who cut up mallows by the bushes, and juniper roots for their meat.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui de convallibus ista rapientes,  cum singula reperissent, ad ea cum clamore currebant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִן־גֵּ֥ו יְגֹרָ֑שׁוּ יָרִ֥יעוּ עָ֝לֵ֗ימוֹ כַּגַּנָּֽב׃
verses.king_james: They were driven forth from among men, (they cried after them as after a thief;)

verses.latin_vulgate: In desertis habitabant torrentium,  et in cavernis terræ, vel super glaream :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּעֲר֣וּץ נְחָלִ֣ים לִשְׁכֹּ֑ן חֹרֵ֖י עָפָ֣ר וְכֵפִֽים׃
verses.king_james: To dwell in the clifts of the valleys, in caves of the earth, and in the rocks.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui inter hujuscemodi lætabantur,  et esse sub sentibus delicias computabant :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֵּין־שִׂיחִ֥ים יִנְהָ֑קוּ תַּ֖חַת חָר֣וּל יְסֻפָּֽחוּ׃
verses.king_james: Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they were gathered together.

verses.latin_vulgate: filii stultorum et ignobilium,  et in terra penitus non parentes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽנֵי־נָ֭בָל גַּם־בְּנֵ֣י בְלִי־שֵׁ֑ם נִ֝כְּא֗וּ מִן־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: They were children of fools, yea, children of base men: they were viler than the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc in eorum canticum versus sum,  et factus sum eis in proverbium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭עַתָּה נְגִינָתָ֣ם הָיִ֑יתִי וָאֱהִ֖י לָהֶ֣ם לְמִלָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: And now am I their song, yea, I am their byword.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abominantur me, et longe fugiunt a me,  et faciem meam conspuere non verentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּ֭עֲבוּנִי רָ֣חֲקוּ מֶ֑נִּי וּ֝מִפָּנַ֗י לֹא־חָ֥שְׂכוּ רֹֽק׃
verses.king_james: They abhor me, they flee far from me, and spare not to spit in my face.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pharetram enim suam aperuit, et afflixit me,  et frenum posuit in os meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־[יִתְרִ֣י] (יתרו) פִ֭תַּח וַיְעַנֵּ֑נִי וְ֝רֶ֗סֶן מִפָּנַ֥י שִׁלֵּֽחוּ׃
verses.king_james: Because he hath loosed my cord, and afflicted me, they have also let loose the bridle before me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad dexteram orientis calamitates meæ illico surrexerunt :  pedes meos subverterunt,  et oppresserunt quasi fluctibus semitis suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־יָמִין֮ פִּרְחַ֢ח יָ֫ק֥וּמוּ רַגְלַ֥י שִׁלֵּ֑חוּ וַיָּסֹ֥לּוּ עָ֝לַ֗י אׇרְח֥וֹת אֵידָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Upon my right hand rise the youth; they push away my feet, and they raise up against me the ways of their destruction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dissipaverunt itinera mea ;  insidiati sunt mihi, et prævaluerunt :  et non fuit qui ferret auxilium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָֽתְס֗וּ נְֽתִיבָ֫תִ֥י לְהַוָּתִ֥י יֹעִ֑ילוּ לֹ֖א עֹזֵ֣ר לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.king_james: They mar my path, they set forward my calamity, they have no helper.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quasi rupto muro, et aperta janua, irruerunt super me,  et ad meas miserias devoluti sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּפֶ֣רֶץ רָחָ֣ב יֶאֱתָ֑יוּ תַּ֥חַת שֹׁ֝אָ֗ה הִתְגַּלְגָּֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: They came upon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselves upon me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Redactus sum in nihilum :  abstulisti quasi ventus desiderium meum,  et velut nubes pertransiit salus mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הׇהְפַּ֥ךְ עָלַ֗י בַּלָּ֫ה֥וֹת תִּרְדֹּ֣ף כָּ֭רוּחַ נְדִבָתִ֑י וּ֝כְעָ֗ב עָבְרָ֥ה יְשֻׁעָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem in memetipso marcescit anima mea,  et possident me dies afflictionis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֗ה עָ֭לַי תִּשְׁתַּפֵּ֣ךְ נַפְשִׁ֑י יֹ֖אחֲז֣וּנִי יְמֵי־עֹֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: And now my soul is poured out upon me; the days of affliction have taken hold upon me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nocte os meum perforatur doloribus,  et qui me comedunt, non dormiunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַ֗יְלָה עֲ֭צָמַי נִקַּ֣ר מֵעָלָ֑י וְ֝עֹרְקַ֗י לֹ֣א יִשְׁכָּבֽוּן׃
verses.king_james: My bones are pierced in me in the night season: and my sinews take no rest.

verses.latin_vulgate: In multitudine eorum consumitur vestimentum meum,  et quasi capitio tunicæ succinxerunt me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּרׇב־כֹּ֭חַ יִתְחַפֵּ֣שׂ לְבוּשִׁ֑י כְּפִ֖י כֻתׇּנְתִּ֣י יַאַזְרֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: By the great force of my disease is my garment changed: it bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Comparatus sum luto,  et assimilatus sum favillæ et cineri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֹרָ֥נִי לַחֹ֑מֶר וָ֝אֶתְמַשֵּׁ֗ל כֶּעָפָ֥ר וָאֵֽפֶר׃
verses.king_james: He hath cast me into the mire, and I am become like dust and ashes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Clamo ad te, et non exaudis me :  sto, et non respicis me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשַׁוַּ֣ע אֵ֭לֶיךָ וְלֹ֣א תַעֲנֵ֑נִי עָ֝מַ֗דְתִּי וַתִּתְבֹּ֥נֶן בִּֽי׃
verses.king_james: I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mutatus es mihi in crudelem,  et in duritia manus tuæ adversaris mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תֵּהָפֵ֣ךְ לְאַכְזָ֣ר לִ֑י בְּעֹ֖צֶם יָדְךָ֣ תִשְׂטְמֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Elevasti me, et quasi super ventum ponens ;  elisisti me valide.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּשָּׂאֵ֣נִי אֶל־ר֭וּחַ תַּרְכִּיבֵ֑נִי וּ֝תְמֹגְגֵ֗נִי (תשוה) [תּוּשִׁיָּֽה]׃
verses.king_james: Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to ride upon it, and dissolvest my substance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scio quia morti trades me,  ubi constituta est domus omni viventi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־יָ֭דַעְתִּי מָ֣וֶת תְּשִׁיבֵ֑נִי וּבֵ֖ית מוֹעֵ֣ד לְכׇל־חָֽי׃
verses.king_james: For I know that thou wilt bring me to death, and to the house appointed for all living.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen non ad consumptionem eorum emittis manum tuam :  et si corruerint, ipse salvabis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֣ךְ לֹֽא־בְ֭עִי יִשְׁלַח־יָ֑ד אִם־בְּ֝פִיד֗וֹ לָהֶ֥ן שֽׁוּעַ׃
verses.king_james: Howbeit he will not stretch out his hand to the grave, though they cry in his destruction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Flebam quondam super eo qui afflictus erat,  et compatiebatur anima mea pauperi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־לֹ֣א בָ֭כִיתִי לִקְשֵׁה־י֑וֹם עָֽגְמָ֥ה נַ֝פְשִׁ֗י לָאֶבְיֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? was not my soul grieved for the poor?

verses.latin_vulgate: Expectabam bona, et venerunt mihi mala :  præstolabar lucem, et eruperunt tenebræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י ט֣וֹב קִ֭וִּיתִי וַיָּ֣בֹא רָ֑ע וַאֲיַחֲלָ֥ה לְ֝א֗וֹר וַיָּ֥בֹא אֹֽפֶל׃
verses.king_james: When I looked for good, then evil came unto me: and when I waited for light, there came darkness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Interiora mea efferbuerunt absque ulla requie :  prævenerunt me dies afflictionis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵעַ֖י רֻתְּח֥וּ וְלֹא־דָ֗מּוּ קִדְּמֻ֥נִי יְמֵי־עֹֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: My bowels boiled, and rested not: the days of affliction prevented me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mœrens incedebam sine furore ;  consurgens, in turba clamabam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קֹדֵ֣ר הִ֭לַּכְתִּי בְּלֹ֣א חַמָּ֑ה קַ֖מְתִּי בַקָּהָ֣ל אֲשַׁוֵּֽעַ׃
verses.king_james: I went mourning without the sun: I stood up, and I cried in the congregation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Frater fui draconum,  et socius struthionum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֭ח הָיִ֣יתִי לְתַנִּ֑ים וְ֝רֵ֗עַ לִבְנ֥וֹת יַעֲנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: I am a brother to dragons, and a companion to owls.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cutis mea denigrata est super me,  et ossa mea aruerunt præ caumate.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ע֭וֹרִי שָׁחַ֣ר מֵעָלָ֑י וְעַצְמִי־חָ֝֗רָה מִנִּי־חֹֽרֶב׃
verses.king_james: My skin is black upon me, and my bones are burned with heat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Versa est in luctum cithara mea,  et organum meum in vocem flentium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י לְ֭אֵבֶל כִּנֹּרִ֑י וְ֝עֻגָבִ֗י לְק֣וֹל בֹּכִֽים׃
verses.king_james: My harp also is turned to mourning, and my organ into the voice of them that weep.
